THE CITY OF "6\ 
~!\~'DIEGO 
110~'O" Street. Room 204 • Sacramento. California. 95814 
:'\ ,\ 
~ 
October 13, 1977 
DEPARTMENT OF 
LEGISLATIVE 
REPRESENTATION 
Mr. Robert Stern 
General Counsel 
Fair Political Practices Comm. 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA. 95814 
Dear~~ 
I would like to have advice from the Fair Political 
Practices Commission regarding the possibility of 
registering an employee as a lobbyist. 
The City of San Diego has three registered lobbyists 
in the Department of Legislative Representation who 
are city employees. 
Our department has a Sacramento 
office which is staffed full-time by a contractual 
employee. 
The salary of this employee is paid by the 
League of California Cities as is the office rent and 
then the entire amount is reimbursed by the City of 
San Diego and is reported as office overhead on the 
"Prop. 9
11 reports. 
Although this employee is carried 
on the League's health plan, that entire amount, 
along with any other benefits such as workers' compen-
sation is reimbursed in full by San Diego. 
This employee 
is therefore considered a contractual employee of the 
City of San Diego. 
Does the procedure to register this contractual employee 
to lobby for the City differ from that of registering 
city employees? 
Is there any specific accounting pro-
cedures that would be required other than those in 
practice by the present city-employee lobbyist? 
Would 
there by any responsibility upon the League of California 
Cities to report because of the contractual arrangement? 
JMW: n d 
Sincerely, 
~ 
John M. Witz 
Legislative

sections.question: Does the procedure to register this contractual employee to lobby for the City differ from that of registering city employees? Is there any specific accounting procedures that would be required other than those in practice by the present city-employee lobbyist? Would there by any responsibility upon the League of California Cities to report because of the contractual arrangement?
sections.question_synthetic: The City of San Diego seeks guidance on whether registering a contractual employee as a lobbyist follows the same procedures as registering city employees, and whether special accounting or reporting requirements apply given that the League of California Cities initially pays salary and benefits before reimbursement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or short answer section present. Document shows some OCR degradation/scanning artifacts but core content is clear. This is dated October 13, 1977, sent to FPPC requesting advice. The document ID (78ADV-78-077) suggests this may be part of a file where FPPC provided a response, but that response is not included in the provided text.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Does the procedure to register this contractual employee to lobby for the City differ from that of registering city employees? Is there any specific accounting procedures that would be required other than those in practice by the present city-employee lobbyist? Would there by any responsibility upon the League of California Cities to report because of the contractual arrangement?

The City of San Diego has three registered lobbyists in the Department of Legislative Representation who are city employees. Our department has a Sacramento office which is staffed full-time by a contractual employee. The salary of this employee is paid by the League of California Cities as is the office rent and then the entire amount is reimbursed by the City of San Diego and is reported as office overhead on the "Prop. 9 11 reports. Although this employee is carried on the League's health plan, that entire amount, along with any other benefits such as workers' compen- sation is reimbursed in full by San Diego. This employee is therefore considered a contractual employee of the City of San Diego. embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the City of San Diego's Department of Legislative Representation asking the FPPC for advice on registering a contractual employee as a lobbyist and clarifying related accounting and reporting responsibilities.
